perturbation.original_text: 1.11 "Complete" or "Completion" or "Completing" means a single operation intended to complete a well as a producer of oil and/or gas in one or more Zone(s), including, but not limited to, the setting of pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.11 "Complete" or "Completion" or "Completing" means the initial operation of setting up a drilling rig on a well. It does not include any operations of setting pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in how 'Completion' is defined. Initially, it includes the act of setting the pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing. The modified definition explicitly excludes these operations, focusing only on the initial setup of the drilling rig. This contradicts later sections referring to Completion Costs and what activities the Company is responsible for if they participate in the completion of the well. This creates an ambiguity to what falls under Completion, and who is responsible of its costs when referring in other sections of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 If BP proposes to Complete the Initial Well and Company timely elects to participate in such Completion attempt in accordance with Section 2.2(A), Company shall pay 8.75% of the Completion Costs associated with the Initial Well and 8.75% of the cost of any newly acquired surface equipment associated with the Initial Well beyond the wellhead connections (including but not limited to stock tanks, separators, treaters, pumping equipment, piping, and metering devices).
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Completion' to exclude activities previously included, such as setting pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing. This directly contradicts Section 2.3, which states that the Company shall pay a percentage of the 'Completion Costs'. The redefined term introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes Completion Costs. This ambiguity creates a direct conflict in determining the financial responsibilities of each party during well completion activities. Because setting pipe, installing wellhead, etc. are no longer part of Completion in the Changed Text, and Completion Costs include those elements in Section 2.3, there's a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.16 "Drilling Costs" means the actual costs and expenses incurred in drilling a well subject to this Agreement beginning with the initiation of preliminary site preparation activities through and including logging, testing and evaluating the well prior to recommending whether to attempt a Completion. Drilling Costs shall include, but shall not be limited to, the costs and expenses associated with permitting, preparing the site, drilling to the Objective Zone, and evaluating any Zone(s) in such well to which a Completion may be attempted, as well as any unplanned or unforeseen events such as fire, explosion, or loss of well control. Drilling Costs shall also include brokerage, abstracting, and reasonable attorney fees related to the preparation of drilling title opinions for such well. Drilling Costs shall not include the cost to plug and abandon any well, including a dry hole, and shall not include any Completion Costs.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.16 "Drilling Costs" means all costs incurred from the moment the contract is signed, including costs to plug and abandon any well, including a dry hole, and shall include Completion Costs. Drilling Costs do not include any other costs and expenses incurred in drilling a well subject to this Agreement beginning with the initiation of preliminary site preparation activities through and including logging, testing and evaluating the well prior to recommending whether to attempt a Completion. Drilling Costs shall not include, but shall not be limited to, the costs and expenses associated with permitting, preparing the site, drilling to the Objective Zone, and evaluating any Zone(s) in such well to which a Completion may be attempted, as well as any unplanned or unforeseen events such as fire, explosion, or loss of well control. Drilling Costs shall also not include brokerage, abstracting, and reasonable attorney fees related to the preparation of drilling title opinions for such well.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Drilling Costs" is changed to include plugging and abandonment costs, as well as completion costs, which were previously excluded. Now the definition excludes everyting that previously belonged to the drilling costs definition. This contradicts Article II, Section 2.6, which states that the costs to plug and abandon the Initial Well will be shared by those who participated in the drilling costs of the Initial Well, implying that Company's share is only for participating parties and now it's for all. This introduces ambiguity and potential disputes on cost allocation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.6 The Initial Well shall be plugged and abandoned in accordance with applicable regulations and at the cost, risk and expense of the parties who participated in the cost of drilling the Initial Well. Company's proportionate share of the cost, risk and expense to plug and abandon the Initial Well shall be eight and three quarters percent (8.75%).
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Drilling Costs" to include plugging and abandonment costs, as well as completion costs, and excludes everything else that previously belonged to the drilling costs definition. This directly contradicts Section 2.6, which states that the costs to plug and abandon the Initial Well will be shared by *those who participated in the cost of drilling the Initial Well*. The change broadens the scope of 'Drilling Costs' to encompass plugging and abandonment and completion costs, therefore impacting the cost allocation and creating an ambiguity by modifying who participates in the plugging and abandonment costs, thus creating a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 If BP does not drill the Initial Well to the Objective Zone, Company shall have the right, but not the obligation, for a period of one (1) year from the date the drilling rig used to drill the Initial Well is removed from the well site location for the Initial Well (the "Rig Release Date"), to participate in the drilling of a Substitute Well. BP shall have no obligation to drill a Substitute Well, and Company shall have no right to propose a Substitute Well. If and when BP elects to drill a Substitute Well, BP shall provide Company with an AFE for the Substitute Well. The AFE for the Substitute Well shall include, at a minimum, the surface and bottomhole location of the Substitute Well, the Objective Zone, and the estimated costs for the Substitute Well as a dry hole and as a producer. Company shall have thirty (30) days from receipt of such written notice to elect whether it shall participate in such Substitute Well. Failure to respond within the time period allowed shall be deemed to be an election not to participate in the Substitute Well.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 If BP does not drill the Initial Well to the Objective Zone, Company shall have the right, but not the obligation, for a period of one (1) year from the date the drilling rig used to drill the Initial Well is removed from the well site location for the Initial Well (the "Rig Release Date"), to participate in the drilling of a Substitute Well. BP shall have an obligation to drill a Substitute Well only if the company request it, and Company shall have the right to propose a Substitute Well. If and when BP elects to drill a Substitute Well, BP shall provide Company with an AFE for the Substitute Well. The AFE for the Substitute Well shall include, at a minimum, the surface and bottomhole location of the Substitute Well, the Objective Zone, and the estimated costs for the Substitute Well as a dry hole and as a producer. Company shall have thirty (30) days from receipt of such written notice to elect whether it shall participate in such Substitute Well. Failure to respond within the time period allowed shall be deemed to be an election not to participate in the Substitute Well.
perturbation.explanation: The change to Section 3.2 alters BP's obligations regarding Substitute Wells. Originally, BP had no obligation to drill a Substitute Well, and Company had no right to propose one. The modified text states BP has an obligation to drill if the company request it, and now the company has the right to propose it. This contradicts the overall agreement's structure, where BP retains operational control, and Company is essentially an investor. Specifically, it contradicts the final sentence of Section 5.3, which assigns exclusive control of well operations to BP, therefore the company can't propose a substitute well if BP has the exclusive control of the well's operations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 The Initial Well and each Substitute Well and Additional Well shall be under the exclusive control of BP and the operation thereof shall be conducted in a prudent and workmanlike manner. BP shall conduct all its activities under this Agreement as a reasonable prudent operator, in a good and workmanlike manner, with due diligence and dispatch, in accordance with good oilfield practice, and in compliance with applicable law and regulation, but in no event shall BP have any liability to Company for losses sustained or liabilities or obligations incurred except such as may result from BP's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3.2 directly contradicts Section 5.3. Originally, BP had exclusive control and no obligation to drill a Substitute Well unless they chose to. The change introduces an obligation for BP to drill a Substitute Well if the Company requests it and grants the Company the right to propose one, which removes BP's exclusive control over well operations stated in Section 5.3. This creates a direct conflict because the company is now able to tell BP to drill a substitute well, removing the exclusivity of control that was assigned to BP in section 5.3.
